rulings.Case Title: RESURGENCE FINANCIAL, LLC VS. JAWANA NICOLE AGUIRRE et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, November 25, 2024, line 1, ASSIGNEE COLLECT ACCESS LLC HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION Hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHATARA SMITH VS. 1635 DIVISADERO MEDICAL BUILDING A LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, November 25, 2024, line 14, DEFENDANT PROFESSIONAL TECHNICAL SECURITY SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Granted. Defendant Professional Technical Security Service (PTSS) has shown ample basis to set aside the default against it per Code of Civil Procedure 473(b). Plaintiff's arguments based on the Gordon Rees firm's asserted lack of authority to represent PTSS in this action lack merit. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LISA STRAUCH EGGERS ET AL VS. DEXTERITY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, November 25, 2024, line 3, DEFENDANT ENJOY TECHNOLOGY, INC.'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Enjoy Technology, Inc.'s demurrer to the first and third causes of action in the third amended complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. The first cause of action sufficiently alleges that the decedent suffered from an uncontrollable impulse to support a claim for wrongful death and the third amended complaint does not show superseding cause as a matter of law. (See, e.g., Whooley v. Tamalpais Union High School District (NC CA 2019) 399 F. Supp. 3d 986). Moving defendant concedes that the asserted procedural deficiency for a survival claim has been remedied. While the first cause of action is not a model of clarity, because the parties understand the intended distinction between the first and third causes of action (the first being wrongful death and the third being survival), no useful purpose is achieved by requiring plaintiffs to file a fourth amended complaint. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ARTI LAL VS. FIRSTSERVICE RESIDENTIAL CALIFORNIA, LLC, A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, November 25, 2024, line 15, DEFENDANT FIRSTSERVICE RESIDENTIAL CALIFORNIA, LLC'S Amended MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION Granted. Even assuming that Code of Civil Procedure 1281.98 applies to the parties' arbitration agreement, defendant did not violate that section because the parties have not completed their contractually required mediation, which is a condition precedent to arbitration. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC VS. MOSS & ASSOCIATES, LLC,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, November 25, 2024, line 13, 2 - DEFENDANT MOSS & ASSOCIATES, LLC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Moss & Associates' anti-SLAPP motion to strike the second cause of action for breach of contract is denied. The filing of a mechanic's lien is not protected activity under the anti-SLAPP statute and, even if it were, plaintiff Kapolei Energy Storage I has shown that there is at least minimal merit to the cause of action sought to be stricken.
